subject: Mathematics
title: Proof by Contradiction
description: A quick, practical guide to Proof by Contradiction.

hook.text: Assume the opposite of what you want to prove. If it leads to nonsense, the original must be true.

buildup.text: Proof by contradiction shows that denying the conclusion creates a logical impossibility.

discovery.text: Euclid proved infinitely many primes this way: assume finitely many, multiply and add 1, find a new prime.

twist.text: Some mathematicians reject contradiction proofs—they show something exists without showing what it is.

climax.text: Despite the controversy, contradiction is one of the most powerful tools in the proof toolkit.

punchline.text: Assume it's false. Watch it break. Conclude it's true.

quiz.question: How does proof by contradiction work?
options: Assume the negation and derive a logical impossibility
